title: erythroxylum macrophyllum var. macrophyllum
intro: Erythroxylum macrophyllum var. macrophyllum, also known as the Andean coca tree, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the Erythroxylaceae family. The leaves of this plant have been used for centuries in traditional medicine and as a stimulant, exhibiting medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Erythroxylum macrophyllum var. macrophyllum include reducing fatigue, improving cognitive function, and alleviating symptoms of altitude sickness, among others. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and extracts, although its use may be subject to local regulations and cultural context.
